title: cronsplain parser: lib/parser.js + tests/test_parser.js (5-field cron -> normalized parse-result shape)
description: Authors the parser AND pins the parse-result SHAPE that explain (T-050), vectors (T-046) and the schedule track consume. Depends on T-044 (require()s lib/errors.js to throw and lib/fields.js for bounds + name maps). This shape is the epic's central contract — keep it simple and stable.
acceptance_criteria: OWNS EXACTLY: projects/cronsplain/lib/parser.js, projects/cronsplain/tests/test_parser.js. Touch no other file. require()s lib/errors.js (CronParseError) and lib/fields.js (bounds + MONTH_NAMES/DOW_NAMES/normalizeDow).

PARSE-RESULT SHAPE (pin exactly; documented in the module docstring since T-046/T-050 read it): parse(expr) -> { fields: { minute:Set<int>, hour:Set<int>, dayOfMonth:Set<int>, month:Set<int>, dayOfWeek:Set<int> }, domRestricted:boolean, dowRestricted:boolean, source:string }. Each Set is the FULLY-EXPANDED set of allowed integers for that field. domRestricted = (day-of-month field text !== '*'); dowRestricted = (day-of-week field text !== '*'). dayOfWeek Set uses 0-6 with 7 normalized to 0. source = the original expression string. DECISION: use Set of ints per field + the two booleans (not a matcher fn) so downstream code is trivial.

Parse 5 whitespace-separated fields. Per field support: single value; range a-b; step */n and a-b/n (n>=1 integer); comma list combining any of these; names (JAN-DEC month=1..12, SUN-SAT dow=0..6, case-insensitive, usable in ranges/lists e.g. MON-FRI, JAN,MAR); wildcard * (expands to [min..max]). dow accepts 0-7 (7->0, and if both 0 and 7 present they collapse).

REJECT with CronParseError (clean message naming the offending field; NO stack leak): field count != 5; value out of the field's [min,max]; reversed range b<a; step n<=0 or non-integer; empty field or empty list element; unknown name; Vixie extensions (@macros, L, W, #, ?, and the bare start-step form a/n such as 3/5); any trailing/garbage token. DECISION+DOCUMENT the reversed-range choice (reject).

tests/test_parser.js (node:test + node:assert): cover every syntax (value, range, */n, a-b/n, list, names incl. case-insensitivity, wildcard); dow 7->0; domRestricted/dowRestricted flags for * vs restricted; expanded-Set correctness for a couple of exprs; and an INVALID-INPUT table (>=8 cases incl. wrong field count, out-of-range, reversed range, step 0, @daily, L, 3/5, unknown name) each asserting assert.throws(()=>parse(x), CronParseError).
SHARED CONVENTIONS: see T-044 acceptance item (CommonJS, zero deps, grammar Q1, DOM/DOW Q2, next Q3, tz Q4). Run from projects/cronsplain/.
HARNESS PROTOCOL: claim/lock(lib/parser.js, tests/test_parser.js)/in_progress/handoff --to-role verifier; NEVER self-mark done; --agent <you> on every call.

VERIFY (commands + output in handoff note): from projects/cronsplain/ `node --test tests/test_parser.js` green; `node -e "const {parse}=require('./lib/parser'); const p=parse('*/15 0 * * 1-5'); console.log([...p.fields.minute].length, p.domRestricted, p.dowRestricted)"` prints "4 false true".

notes.note: plan: implement lib/parser.js (parse(expr) -> {fields:{minute,hour,dayOfMonth,month,dayOfWeek}:Set<int>, domRestricted, dowRestricted, source}) per pinned shape; per-field tokenizer for value/range/step/list/names/wildcard; reject Vixie extensions + malformed input via CronParseError; write tests/test_parser.js covering all syntax + >=8 invalid cases.

VERIFY REPLAY: (1) cd projects/cronsplain && node --test tests/test_parser.js -> 'tests 24 / pass 24 / fail 0', exit 0. (2) cd projects/cronsplain && node --test tests/*.js -> 'tests 38 / pass 38 / fail 0' (14 from test_fields.js + 24 from test_parser.js), exit 0. (3) cd projects/cronsplain && node -e "const {parse}=require('./lib/parser'); const p=parse('*/15 0 * * 1-5'); console.log([...p.fields.minute].length, p.domRestricted, p.dowRestricted)" -> prints exactly '4 false true' (matches acceptance criteria verbatim). (4) python3 .harness/bin/goal_mode.py run --task T-045 --cmd "cd projects/cronsplain && node --test tests/*.js" -> 'GOAL REACHED ... on iteration 1/3'. Confirmed via git status that only projects/cronsplain/lib/parser.js and projects/cronsplain/tests/test_parser.js were created; lib/errors.js, lib/fields.js, package.json, tests/test_fields.js (T-044 owned) are byte-identical/untouched.

HANDOFF -> verifier: READY FOR VERIFICATION. Owned files created (untouched otherwise): projects/cronsplain/lib/parser.js, projects/cronsplain/tests/test_parser.js. PARSE-RESULT SHAPE (pinned, matches acceptance criteria exactly, documented in parser.js module docstring): parse(expr) -> { fields: { minute:Set<int>, hour:Set<int>, dayOfMonth:Set<int>, month:Set<int>, dayOfWeek:Set<int> }, domRestricted:boolean, dowRestricted:boolean, source:string }. Sets are fully-expanded ints; domRestricted/dowRestricted are literal-text checks (rawField !== '*'); dayOfWeek Set is 0-6 (7 normalized to 0 on insertion, 0+7-both-present collapse to one entry); source is the verbatim original expression string (untrimmed). REPLAY COMMANDS + OBSERVED OUTPUT (all from projects/cronsplain/): (1)  -> tests 24, pass 24, fail 0, exit 0. (2)  -> tests 38, pass 38, fail 0, exit 0 (14 from T-044's test_fields.js + 24 here) -- use this form, NOT bare 'node --test' (T-044's documented false-green: bare form silently reports 0/0/0 on node v24). (3)  -> prints '4 false true' exactly as pinned in the acceptance criteria. (4) GOAL REACHED: 'cd projects/cronsplain && node --test tests/*.js' succeeded on iteration 1/3 (0.06s). -> GOAL REACHED iteration 1/3. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA CHECKLIST: [1] owns exactly the 2 files, requires ./errors (CronParseError) and ./fields (FIELDS/MONTH_NAMES/DOW_NAMES/normalizeDow) -- yes, no other file touched (git status confirms). [2] parse-result shape pinned + documented verbatim in docstring -- yes. [3] 5-field grammar (value/range/step */n+a-b/n/list/names JAN-DEC+SUN-SAT case-insensitive/wildcard, dow 0-7 with 7->0 collapse) -- yes, covered by 'single value/range/step/list/wildcard/names/case-insensitivity/name-ranges/7->0/0+7-collapse' tests. [4] rejects field-count!=5, out-of-range, reversed range, bad step, empty field/list element, unknown name, Vixie extensions (@,L,W,#,?,bare a/n), trailing garbage, each with CronParseError+.field where field-scoped -- yes, 23-case invalid table (>= required 8) asserts instanceof CronParseError, .expression, and .field per case. [5] tests cover every syntax + dow 7->0 + domRestricted/dowRestricted flags (all 4 combinations) + expanded-Set correctness (2 full exprs) + invalid table -- yes, 38 total assertions across 24 new test() blocks. [6] shared conventions (CommonJS, zero deps, run from projects/cronsplain/) -- yes, no require()s beyond node:test/node:assert/../lib/errors/../lib/fields. [7] harness protocol (claim/lock/in_progress/handoff, never self-done) -- followed. [8] VERIFY command output matches verbatim -- confirmed above. DEVIATIONS: none from the acceptance criteria text itself. One documented DESIGN NOTE (not a deviation, just disclosed reasoning): a literal zero-length field token cannot actually be produced by the trimmed+whitespace-collapsing tokenizer, so the 'empty field' rejection rule is exercised via its reachable manifestation (empty list elements: '1,,5', trailing/leading commas) rather than a literal empty top-level field; the defensive empty-fieldText guard remains in the code and is documented as such in the docstring. Locks on both owned files released after this handoff.
